title: Stay in dialect
desc: They keep switching to High German — steer back
setting: A shopkeeper hears your slight accent and helpfully switches to High German. You'd rather stay in dialect.

steps.speaker: Verchäuferin
steps.es: (auf Hochdeutsch) Ah, guten Tag! Was darf es denn sein?
steps.en: (in High German) Ah, hello! What can I get you?

options.es: Grüezi! Sie chönd ruhig Dialäkt rede, ich verstah's guet.
options.en: Hello! You can speak dialect, I understand it well.
options.feedback: Friendly and clear — most people happily switch back when invited.

options.es: (auf Hochdeutsch) Guten Tag, ich nehme ein Brot.
options.en: (in High German) Hello, I'll take a bread.
options.feedback: You matched her, which is fine — but staying in dialect is how you grow.

options.es: Reded Sie nöd so mit mir.
options.en: Don't talk to me like that.
options.feedback: She was being kind — a gentle nudge works far better than a rebuke.

steps.speaker: Verchäuferin
steps.es: Oh, gärn! Die meischte wänd ja, dass ich Hochdütsch rede.
steps.en: Oh, gladly! Most people want me to speak High German.

options.es: Ich nöd. Ich wott's grad lärne, drum häre.
options.en: Not me. I want to learn it, that's why.
options.feedback: Honest and motivated — she'll enjoy helping you now.
Charming, but a word on why builds the connection further.
Putting down the standard language is needlessly harsh — keep it positive.

steps.speaker: Verchäuferin
steps.es: Das find ich schön. Also, was häsch gärn?
steps.en: I think that's lovely. So, what would you like?

options.es: Es Ruchbrot und zwöi Gipfeli, bitte.
options.en: A wholegrain bread and two croissants, please.
options.feedback: Natural and local — and notice she's now using 'du' with you.
Opens a friendly exchange — a great way to practise more dialect.
A bit vague — but it'll get you a friendly suggestion at least.

steps.speaker: Verchäuferin
steps.es: Gärn. Und du redsch ja würkli guet, alli Achtig!
steps.en: Gladly. And you really speak well, hats off!

options.es: Merci viilmal, das freut mi. Schöne Tag no!
options.en: Thank you very much, that makes me happy. Have a nice day!
options.feedback: Gracious and warm — you held the dialect and won her over. Perfect.
Modest and likeable — a lovely way to take a compliment.
A little too self-deprecating — you earned that praise, let it land.
